data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: JNJ, MS, PLTR, TRIP
data.description:
Recent analyst actions include notable calls on stocks including Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Morgan Stanley (MS) receiving upgrade. On the downside, Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is being downgraded by Mike Zaccardi on valuation concerns following a 50% rally, and Tripadvisor (TRIP) is cut to Sell by Gary Alexander due to deteriorating fundamentals and competitive pressures.

Upgrades

  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Envision Research. The analyst points to a drastic slowdown in insider selling activity and upcoming Q4 earnings catalysts, including AI-driven government contracts and potential defense budget increases, as reasons for the more bullish outlook.

    “The pace of insider selling has slowed drastically, a change too large to ignore in my view. Combined with the latest EPS revisions and business development (especially with the potential defense budget increase), I now see a more skewed return/risk curve for PLTR now, leading to a rating upgrade to buy.”

  • Morgan Stanley (MS): Upgrade Sell to Hold by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst acknowledges being overly critical previously, citing strong Q4 fundamentals with 10% revenue growth and 21% EPS growth, though valuation concerns prevent a more bullish rating.

    “In hindsight, my sell rating was too harsh for a solid firm like Morgan Stanley. As stated just above, despite some hiccups here and there in their Q4, the company’s fundamentals are looking good. However, ‘good’ may not be enough to fully justify the current valuation. Morgan Stanley is now at its most expensive valuation in recent memory.”

Downgrades

  • Johnson & Johnson (JNJ): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Mike Zaccardi, CFA, CMT. The analyst cites stretched valuation following a 50% rally over the past 12 months, with shares now trading above 21x forward EPS and offering limited margin of safety despite solid fundamentals.

    “JNJ now trades at more than 21 times expected EPS. That’s a near-market multiple and more than a full turn pricier compared to its long-term average. I simply think that’s stretched. … If we assume a generous 20x multiple and $11.50 of current-year non-GAAP EPS, then shares should trade near $230. That just doesn’t leave much of a margin of safety anymore.”

  • Tripadvisor (TRIP): Downgrade Hold to Sell by Gary Alexander. The analyst points to deteriorating fundamentals and loss of competitiveness in the OTA market, with Tripadvisor’s core segment growth turning negative while peers like Expedia, Booking Holdings, and Airbnb accelerate bookings growth.

    “It’s not difficult here to grasp that the three main OTA companies are seeing accelerating bookings while Tripadvisor has shown sharp deceleration to a negative y/y decline. Tripadvisor has a problem with market share, not with the overall travel economy. … This is no longer a stock worth holding onto: lock in losses and move on elsewhere.”

data.title: The Norfolk Southern-Union Pacific merger hits a regulatory bump but is still on track
data.description:
The Surface Transportation Board weighed in that the Union Pacific (UNP)-Norfolk Southern (NSC) major merger application filed in December is incomplete and therefore was rejected without prejudice. In a unanimous decision, the Board found the applicants failed to provide required post-consummation market share projections reflecting anticipated traffic growth and diversions. The STB also determined that the related bid for control of the Terminal Railroad Association of St. Louis is a significant, not minor, transaction and flagged several technical difficulties. 

The STB said the applicants must inform the board by February 17 if and when they plan to submit a revised application.

Weighing in on the development, Bank of America said the delay appears par for the course in a need for more information in the transcontinental railroad merger proposal. "We believe UNP will resubmit, with its timeline pushed back a few months, and closure could be extended to mid-2027. We do not believe this derails the app, yet it reinforces the STB’s desire for complete analysis," wrote Ken Hoexter. The firm sees the STB decision as a pause, not a change in course. BofA reiterated its Buy and $266 price objective on Union Pacific (UNP).

Shares of Union Pacific (UNP) opened 2.0% lower on Tuesday. Norfolk Southern (NSC) was down 2.7%.

data.title: Crypto funds record inflows of $2.17B last week: report
data.description:
Cryptocurrency investment products reported inflows of $2.17B for the week ending January 19, marking the largest weekly inflows since October 2025, with Bitcoin (BTC-USD) leading the inflows.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) ETFs recorded inflows of $1.55B last week, according to a CoinShare report. 

“Inflows were stronger earlier in the week, but sentiment turned negative on Friday, with $378M of outflows following diplomatic escalation over Greenland and renewed threats of additional tariffs. Sentiment was also weighed down by suggestions that Kevin Hassett, a leading contender for the next US Fed Chair and a well-known policy dove, is likely to remain in his current role,” James Butterfill, CoinShares’ Head of Research, said.

According to the report, Ethereum (ETH-USD) and Solana (SOL-USD) recorded inflows of $496M and $45.5M, respectively, despite proposals under the CLARITY Act from the U.S. Senate Banking Committee that could restrict stablecoins from offering yield.

Altcoins such as XRP, Sui, Lido, and Hedera also registered inflows totaling $69.5M, $5.7M, $3.7M, and $2.6M, respectively

Last week, the U.S. led inflows totaling $2.05B, followed by Germany with $63.9M, Switzerland with $41.6M, Canada with $12.3M, and the Netherlands with $6M.

data.title: Keyera plunges after Alberta Envirofuels facility hit by outage until May
data.description:
Keyera (KEYUF) fell nearly 4% in Toronto on Monday after saying it shut down its Alberta Envirofuels facility due to an extended unplanned outage because of the failure of a key component.

The company identified component failure involving long-life equipment that had been replaced some three years ago as part of AEF's ongoing maintenance and reliability programs.

Keyera (KEYUF) said it expects the facility will return to service in May 2026, citing the expected timing required for the fabrication, delivery and installation of replacement components.

The company now plans to complete a previously planned six-week major turnaround, scheduled for fall 2026, during the current outage period, eliminating the need for a separate shutdown later.

Keyera (KEYUF) said it anticipates a total impact on FY 2026 realized margin in its marketing segment of $110M and realized margin in its liquids infrastructure segment of $15M, while expecting maintenance capital to rise by ~$10M, raising guidance for 2026 up to $140M-$160M.

data.title: Organon gains on report of Sun Pharmaceutical interest
data.description:
Organon (OGN) rose 6.5% after a report that India's Sun Pharmaceutical made a non-binding offer to acquire the company. 

Sun Pharmaceutical has tied up acquisition financing for a deal in the form of bridge loans of about $10B to $14B, according to a report from the Economic Times.  The due diligence process is expected to start shortly, after which a binding bid will be made. 

The offer is expected to be all cash, according to the report. 

Sun Pharma and Organon didn't respond to the Economic Times request for comment.

data.title: Fastenal shares slide after Q4 results show margin pressure
data.description:
Fastenal (FAST) shares fell 3.7% in the first hour of regular trading Tuesday after the industrial distributor reported fourth-quarter earnings that met Wall Street expectations but showed signs of margin pressure and slowing momentum in parts of the business.

The company (FAST) reported diluted earnings of $0.26 a share for the quarter, matching the consensus estimate. Revenue rose 11.1% from a year earlier to $2.03 billion, broadly in line with analyst expectations of about $2.04 billion.

Net income rose to $294.1 million, or $0.26 a share, from $262.1 million, or $0.23 a share, a year earlier.

Despite the headline beats on growth, investors appeared to focus on weaker underlying profitability trends. Gross margin slipped to 44.3% from 44.8% a year earlier as higher cost of goods sold, timing of supplier rebates and modestly negative price-cost dynamics weighed on results. Management also cited a continued mix shift toward larger customers, which typically carry lower margins.

Fastenal (FAST) said industrial production remained sluggish during the quarter, with overall sales growth still relying heavily on price increases of roughly 310 to 340 basis points rather than volume alone. While unit sales increased, the company acknowledged that broader business activity remains subdued, particularly among smaller, non-contract customers.

Another potential concern for investors was a slowdown in the pace of technology deployments. Weighted FASTBin and FASTVend signings declined 12.1% year over year in the fourth quarter, and full-year signings fell 7.5%, even as installations and sales through the FMI platform continued to grow. The company expects signings to reaccelerate in 2026 but offered no near-term timeline.

Customer site data also pointed to softer demand at the lower end of the market. The total number of active customer sites fell to 92,136 from 100,435 a year earlier, reflecting declines in smaller-spend customers despite gains among higher-spending accounts.

Operating income margin edged up slightly to 19.0%, helped by lower net interest expense and SG&A leverage, but that improvement was not enough to offset concerns about margin durability and the sustainability of recent pricing gains. The company did not disclose quarterly earnings, before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, but profitability trends suggest limited near-term expansion in ebitda margins.

Fastenal (FAST) continued to return capital to shareholders through dividends, though it did not repurchase shares during the year. Total debt declined to $125 million at year-end, down from $200 million a year earlier.

data.title: Bitmine Immersion reports ~35K Ethereum acquisitions in the past week
data.description:
Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) on Tuesday announced the acquisition of 35,268 Ethereum in the past week, bringing its ETH holdings to 4.20M tokens.

Shares were -6.96% pre-market to $28.99. ETH-USD was 3.80% lower to $3,066.91 before the U.S. market open.

The Las Vegas-based blockchain technology company now owns 3.48% of the ETH token supply.

The company's staked ETH stands at 1,838,003.

Bitmine said its crypto + total cash + "moonshots" holdings total $14.5B. As of January 19, the company's crypto holdings were comprised of 4.20M ETH acquired at $3,211 per ETH and 193 bitcoins.

Additionally, the company held a $22M stake in Eightco Holdings (ORBS) and cash of $979M.

For the January 15 stockholders meeting, the company said its proposal to increase authorized shares was passed with 81% of votes cast "yes".

The figure represents 52.2% of the total outstanding shares voting in favor of the increase.

data.title: Vulcan Value Partners adds RYAN, TRU, exits LVMHF, PDRDF among Q4 moves
data.description:
Vulcan Value Partners purchased Ryan Specialty Holdings (RYAN) and TransUnion (TRU) during the fourth quarter.

During the quarter, the fund sold Skyworks Solutions (SWKS), SS&C Technologies (SSNC), LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE (LVMHF), and Pernod Ricard (PDRDF).

In its quarterly fund letter, the fund mentioned that it sold LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE and Pernod Ricard to reallocate capital into more discounted companies and further improve the margin of safety of the portfolio.

Source: Quarterly fund letter
